candidate_pkrs.contribution: Reshape PR 346 activation ownership, explicit dependencies, cancellation, cleanup, and observation without discarding its proven domain contracts.
Add the bounded GitHub issue queue, code, PostgreSQL, Victoria, verifier, and publication proof after queue and evidence contracts are accepted.

confidence_probability_update.reason: The current declarations, docs, tests, and provider adapters were grouped into a full public-concept inventory. A saved 39-marker replay pins the conclusion to the current PR head. Replacement details remain outside this DKR.

payload.decision: candidate only; continue PR 346, preserve the listed contracts, and reshape the activation and lifecycle spine through DKR-1 to DKR-5; never accepted by this worker
payload.decision_target: keep, reshape, and remove map for PR 346
payload.flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed, keep downstream advance blocked, and request a fresh DKR-0 replay when HEAD, any evidence hash, or any inventory marker changes.
questions_answered: PR 346 should continue rather than be replaced because its durable records, authority contract, role and tool declarations, validation engine, effect ports, provider attempt seam, and database-analysis proof remain useful.
The activation and lifecycle spine must be reshaped before the issue-triage slice is added.
Manual session pre-resolution is a public footgun: first resolution inside session.run creates a temporary current-owned runtime whose state is not visible to later sibling execution.
Context cleanup currently calls Runtime.shutdown, which calls beginFinish and changes both live and record status to finishing without an explicit finish flow.

Session run and boundary flows manually fan out session, authority, epoch, branch, work, attempt, and abort tags. The runtime facts remain required; only their activation binding and repeated propagation need later ergonomic proof.

Required runtime tags and eager activation of the complete statically declared role, tool, and skill tree are intended strengths. Missing-tag runtime failure is accepted, and static missing-tag analysis is deferred.
SessionEvent remains valuable domain history but does not replace a safe extension-visible context observation model.
The current tests already seed same-scope session isolation, read-only parallel database analysis, readiness-before-effect, wait and resume, provider streaming, and packed namespace behavior.

The replacement must preserve authority narrowing, readiness, schema identity, semantic work settlement, wait and wake, fork and merge, steering fences, explicit checkpoint and finish, provider continuation isolation, and the scope seam.
questions_unanswered: DKR-1 must decide the exact activation resource ownership and binding API after testing controller(flow).exec({ input, tags }) without pre-resolution.
DKR-2 must decide the smallest generic ExecutionContext cancellation and descendant-settlement contract and whether any Runtime controller remains for targeted work steering.
DKR-3 must decide the safe observation projection and terminal event contract for extensions.
DKR-4 must decide whether awaited controller executions and a fixed worker loop are enough for queue watching without spawn or start.
DKR-5 must decide the exact evidence, freshness, independent verification, authority, and idempotency contract for GitHub publication.
Independent validation must confirm that the grouped inventory covers every public concept and that each judgement-heavy disposition follows from the cited evidence.
payload.read_method: Run the saved inventory checker at the pinned PR head, then inspect the grouped public-symbol and workaround dispositions with their file and line references.
payload.reviewer_audit_status: pending independent validator review; this writer did not accept its own classifications
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: Keeping the current PR unchanged would retain a mandatory pre-resolution ordering rule and temporary-session state loss.
Calling beginFinish from cleanup risks treating ordinary activation close as a durable business transition even when it does not commit.
Framework dependencies added by tool, skill, and role helpers remain declared in the returned resource graph and are not defects. Hidden physical effect edges remain forbidden.
Removing the Runtime wholesale would lose semantic work, branch, permit, steering, artifact, memory, schedule, and continuation behavior that the issue-triage example needs.
Adding spawn, start, a worker pool, or another resource ownership mode before DKR-1, DKR-2, and DKR-4 would create a primitive proof gap.
Publishing SessionEvent payloads as extension observations without a safe projection would risk sensitive context export.
payload.source_of_truth: current feature worktree and saved replay script

reasons: This worker cannot accept its own public API classifications.

Every active anti-goal still requires a fresh independent validator record.

confidence_probability_update.reason: The first candidate overstated confidence because marker coverage was not disposition coverage. V2 proves the complete changed-path and concept matrix mechanically, but architectural judgement remains pending independent review.

payload.decision: candidate only; continue PR 346 using the v2 keep, reshape, and remove matrix as steering input, with architectural judgement pending independent review
payload.decision_target: complete keep, reshape, and remove map for every path and mixed public concept changed by PR 346
payload.flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed and rerun DKR-0 when HEAD, main, the changed-path universe, a matrix row, an evidence hash, or the independent review state changes.
questions_answered: The current PR universe is exactly 58 paths from git diff --name-status main...HEAD at d8dc7e845d3a7618fdf41525b67b8bac83d05d28.
Every changed path has exactly one top-level disposition, at least one known rule, evidence tied to that path, at least one public concept, and downstream owner DKRs.
The matrix has 23 top-level keep rows and 35 top-level reshape rows. No whole changed file is removed.
The matrix has 88 concept-level rows: 47 keep, 36 reshape, and 5 remove. Mixed source files cannot hide concept-level decisions behind one file label.
The five removals are the manual pre-resolution behavior, cleanup-to-finish transition, and SDK abort-tag bridges; their behavior is assigned to DKR-1 or DKR-2 before product edits.
Eager activation of the full statically declared tree, runtime-provided required tags, and runtime missing-tag failure are kept. Static missing-tag analysis remains deferred.
The checker rejects nine bad matrix classes: missing, duplicate, out-of-universe, stale, absent rule, unknown rule, absent evidence, unassigned owner, and collapsed mixed concepts.
Mechanical matrix coverage and architectural judgement are separate. The matrix judgement status is pending_independent_review and judgement_acceptance_count is zero.
The prior validator rejection is retained as evidence instead of being overwritten or treated as approval.
questions_unanswered: A separate validator must inspect whether each of the 88 concept dispositions follows from the cited product evidence and the confirmed frame.
DKR-1 must decide the accepted activation anchoring and deactivation cleanup contract before X-PRE-RESOLVE and X-CLEANUP-FINISH become progression work.
DKR-2 must decide the reusable context cancellation and descendant-settlement contract before X-ABORT-TAG becomes progression work.
DKR-3 must decide the safe extension projection and its relation to domain SessionEvent records.
DKR-4 must decide the bounded queue-watcher composition without assuming spawn or start.
DKR-5 must decide the evidence, freshness, independent verification, authority, and idempotent GitHub publication contract.
payload.read_method: Run the v2 checker to derive the 58-path universe and validate all file, rule, evidence, owner, and mixed-concept rows; then require a separate validator to inspect the architectural judgements.
payload.reviewer_audit_status: v1 rejected by validator-dkr-0-1; v2 mechanical gaps repaired; v2 architectural judgement pending a new independent validator
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The v1 marker checker is retained but no longer used as disposition-completeness evidence.
The 58-path matrix prevents README, changelog, package, provider, test-kit, build, workspace, and consumer changes from disappearing behind core-only evidence.
Concept-level rows preserve eager declared-tree activation and runtime tag binding while isolating activation, cancellation, observation, queue, and publication changes.
The cleanup-to-finishing breach remains discovery work under DKR-1; this checkpoint does not authorize a product fix.
No spawn, start, worker-pool, new ownership mode, or static missing-tag analyzer is promoted by this checkpoint.
payload.source_of_truth: git diff --name-status main...HEAD, the pinned v2 disposition matrix, the saved v2 replay checker, and the prior independent rejection

reasons: The v2 checker proves complete mechanical coverage and negative-case rejection, not architectural correctness.
This writer cannot accept its own 88 concept dispositions.
A new independent validator must inspect the judgement rows and replay every numeric wall before the orchestrator may advance.
